title: The Pillay Family Trail: Riverside Wildlife Detectives
blurb: A secret wild lake hides right on the edge of Guildford! Become Wildlife Detectives across reedbeds and ponds — stomp the long wooden boardwalk, sneak into a bird hide, name a riverside lock and hunt for dragonflies. Flat, shady-by-the-water and lovely and cool on a hot day. Isaac cracks the clues, Eli spots the wildlife. ~2.8 km, flexes 45–90 min.
difficulty: family · ages 2 & 6 · flat wetland paths + boardwalk · buggy-friendly · flexes 45–90 min

start.name: Riverside Nature Reserve car park, Bowers Lane, Burpham (GU4 7ND)
start.directions: ~8 min drive NE of central Guildford. Small car park (~20 spaces) at the end of Bowers Lane, Burpham (GU4 7ND). Flat paths plus an ~800 m wooden boardwalk over the marsh — buggy-friendly, though check the boardwalk width for the pushchair (a sling for Eli is handy). ⚠️ SAFETY: open water and reedbeds everywhere — hold little hands near the lake, locks and boardwalk edges. Cooler than open ground thanks to the water and trees. No café; bring a picnic and water.

stops.name: The Secret Lake
stops.clue: Hidden right next to the busy road is a big, quiet LAKE full of wildlife. Find the water's edge and check in, Wildlife Detectives. How many different birds can you spot on the water?
stops.little_ones: Eli — can you find the big water and point at a duck or a swan? 🦢
stops.grownup: Stoke Lake sits in the 80-hectare Riverside Nature Reserve — a Green Flag reserve of lake, reedbed, ponds, meadow and woodland squeezed between the River Wey and the A3.
stops.story: This big lake is the heart of the Riverside Nature Reserve — a secret wild place on the edge of town, home to loads of birds, bugs and frogs.

stops.name: The Wobbly Boardwalk
stops.clue: To cross the squishy marsh without getting muddy boots, there's a long wooden BOARDWALK on stilts — over 800 metres of it! Walk out onto it and check in. Listen for frogs and look for fish below.
stops.little_ones: Eli — stomp along the wooden walkway: clomp, clomp, clomp! Can you see your reflection in the water? 🐸
stops.grownup: The reserve's boardwalk runs ~800 m across the wetland and reedbeds, letting you explore the marsh without disturbing it (or sinking!).
stops.story: This long wooden boardwalk lets you walk right over the marsh and reeds without getting stuck in the mud — perfect for spotting frogs and dragonflies!

stops.name: The Secret Bird Hide
stops.clue: Find the little wooden hut with long thin windows — it's a BIRD HIDE, where you hide to watch birds without scaring them away. When you're inside, should you (a) shout and bang, or (b) stay quiet and peek?
stops.answer_hint: Birds fly off if you're loud… so you must be very, very…?
stops.little_ones: Eli — tiptoe to the hut and put your finger on your lips: shhhh! 🤫
stops.grownup: The reserve's bird hide overlooks the lake and reedbed — good for herons, grebes and ducks, plus dragonflies in summer. Stay quiet and still and the wildlife comes to you.
stops.story: A bird hide is a secret hut with little windows. Keep quiet and still inside and the birds won't know you're there — so you can watch them up close!

stops.name: Stoke Lock
stops.clue: At the far end, where the reserve meets the River Wey, is a pretty LOCK — a watery lift that raises and lowers boats. Find its name sign. What is this lock called? (Clue: it shares its name with the lake and the park nearby.)
stops.answer_hint: Same name as 'Stoke' Lake and Stoke Park…
stops.little_ones: Eli — find the big wooden gates and watch the water. Can you spot a boat? ⛵
stops.grownup: Stoke Lock is one of the older locks on the 1653 Wey Navigation. Cross the footbridge here to join the towpath that leads all the way to Dapdune Wharf and into Guildford.
stops.story: Stoke Lock lifts boats up and down the River Wey. Cross the bridge here and the towpath would take you all the way into town!

stops.name: Reedbed Minibeast Hunt
stops.clue: The reeds and ponds are buzzing with little creatures in summer. Which of these would you really find zooming about here: dragonflies, or dinosaurs?
stops.answer_hint: Not the giant scary ones from millions of years ago — the little zippy ones with see-through wings!
stops.little_ones: Eli — flap like a dragonfly and see if you can spot one zooming over the water! 🦋
stops.grownup: The reserve's reedbeds and ponds teem with dragonflies, damselflies, butterflies, frogs and newts through summer — a brilliant minibeast hunt for little ones.
stops.story: In summer the reeds are full of dragonflies, butterflies and buzzing bugs. How many tiny creatures can you spot before you head home?

finale.title: Wildlife Detectives Isaac & Eli — case solved! 🦆🎉
finale.message: You found the secret lake, braved the wobbly boardwalk, hid in the bird hide, named Stoke Lock and hunted for minibeasts. Brilliant nature detectives! Show Mum and Dad all your stickers.
finale.reward_idea: A picnic by the lake, or an ice cream on the way home! 🍦

credits: Map data © OpenStreetMap contributors · facts © Guildford Borough Council / Thames Basin Heaths Partnership
_recce_notes: PREP 2026-06-20, close-to-home option. Start = Riverside Nature Reserve car park, end of Bowers Lane, Burpham GU4 7ND (~20 spaces; pin 51.2640,-0.5625 is the POSTCODE CENTROID — FOOT-VERIFY the actual car-park entrance). Flat, mostly buggy-friendly (paths + ~800 m boardwalk; check boardwalk width for the pushchair, sling for Eli handy); water everywhere so hold little hands; cooler than open ground. SOFT pins to FOOT-VERIFY + retune geofences: Stoke Lake (1), boardwalk (2), bird hide (3 — confirm location), reedbed (5). Stoke Lock (4) ~51.2533,-0.5677 (GU1 1QE area) reasonably anchored — confirm the name sign. Open wetland = good GPS. Durable clues: bird-hide behaviour + Stoke Lock name. Alt access: Stoke Lock (Woking Rd GU1 1QE) or behind Spectrum (Parkway GU1 1UP).
